doctrine: local-first, no harvesting, explicit consent, no medical advice

datasets.name: OpenDiabetic Local NAS Medical Dataset Inventory
datasets.description: Local Synology/NFS medical dataset holdings. Do not redistribute raw data. Generate manifests, checksums, schema summaries, and approved developer access packs.
datasets.license_notes: Treat every local dataset as restricted until source, license, consent, and PHI status are documented.

datasets.name: CDC NHANES
datasets.description: National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey questionnaires, datasets, and documentation including demographics, dietary, examination, laboratory, and questionnaire files.
datasets.license_notes: Use CDC/NCHS data user agreement and analytic guidelines. Limited-access files require separate controls.

datasets.name: CDC BRFSS
datasets.description: Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System health-related telephone survey data covering risk behaviors, chronic conditions, and preventive service use.
datasets.license_notes: Use CDC documentation, questionnaires, weighting guidance, and public data-use expectations.

datasets.name: UCI Diabetes 130-US Hospitals 1999-2008
datasets.description: UCI dataset of 101,766 hospital encounters for patients diagnosed with diabetes, used for readmission/classification research.
datasets.license_notes: UCI lists CC BY 4.0; attribution required. Dataset page states sensitive attributes include age, gender, and race.

datasets.name: PhysioNet MIMIC-IV
datasets.description: Large deidentified EHR dataset for ICU and emergency department admissions. Access requires PhysioNet credentialing and agreement.
datasets.license_notes: PhysioNet states derived datasets/models should be treated as sensitive and shared under the same agreement as source data.

datasets.name: OpenDiabetic Vault-Derived Local Examples
datasets.description: User-owned local records, reminders, care packs, and support workflow examples generated from a LocalDiabetic NAS vault only with explicit consent.
datasets.license_notes: User-owned. Research export requires explicit opt-in, de-identification plan, receipt, and revocation path.
